data.command: export PATH="$HOME/go/bin:$HOME/bin:$PATH" && gt prime --hook && gt mail check --inject

message.content:
[GAS TOWN] deacon <- daemon • 2026-02-22T19:01 • patrol

I am Deacon. Start patrol: run gt deacon heartbeat, then check gt hook. If no hook, create mol-deacon-patrol wisp and execute it.

content.thinking:
Multiple completed patrol wisps are stacked up. The hooked wisp (skogtown-wisp-wisp-037x) also has all steps complete. I need to squash the completed patrol and start a fresh one.

Let me squash the current patrol and create a new one.

content.text: Multiple completed patrols stacked. Squashing and creating fresh patrol.

There are 4 processes running:
1. Witness for rig "claude" - started at 09:55
2. Refinery for rig "claude" - started at 09:55
3. Refinery for rig "aldervall_quiz" - started at 11:27

Actually, these agents are running but the rigs aren't registered in the town status. 

The processes are still attached to their rig directories and were spawned by the deacon, so they might be running intentionally even though the rigs were removed from registration. I shouldn't terminate them without understanding whether this is expected behavior, especially since I'm not responsible for managing the witness/refinery processes themselves.
